# IMIDUS Branding Assets Installation Summary

**Date:** 2026-03-12
**Generated by:** Novatech
**Applies to:** TOAST project at `/home/kali/Desktop/TOAST`

## Overview
This document summarizes the successful integration of the Imidus branding package into the TOAST project. All files have been copied, configurations updated, and dependencies installed according to the `IMIDUS Branding Assets — Fixed Installation Guide`.

## Platforms Updated

### 1. React Native Mobile App (`src/mobile/ImidusCustomerApp`)
- **Assets:** Logo PNGs copied to `src/assets/images/` (mapped from branding package names).
- **Theme:** `colors.ts`, `typography.ts`, `spacing.ts`, `images.ts`, and `index.ts` created in `src/theme/`.
- **Components:** `ImidusHeader.tsx` and `LoyaltyCard.tsx` copied to `src/components/common/`.
- **Android Configuration:**
  - `colors.xml` and `styles.xml` updated in `android/app/src/main/res/values/`.
  - `splash_background.xml` and `splash.png` copied to `android/app/src/main/res/drawable/`.
  - `AndroidManifest.xml` updated to use `SplashTheme`.
  - `MainActivity.kt` updated to switch to `AppTheme` in `onCreate()`.
- **iOS Configuration:**
  - `LaunchScreen.storyboard` updated with brand blue background (#1E5AA8).
  - `splash.png` copied to `ios/ImidusCustomerApp/Images.xcassets/Splash.imageset/`.
  - `Contents.json` created for Splash imageset.
- **Path Aliases:**
  - `tsconfig.json` updated with `@/theme`, `@/components/*`, `@/assets/*` paths.
  - `babel.config.js` updated with `module-resolver` plugin.
- **Dependencies:** `babel-plugin-module-resolver` installed via `pnpm`.

### 2. Web Ordering Site (`src/web`)
- **Assets:** Logo PNGs copied to `public/images/`.
- **Global CSS:** `globals.css` (branding version) replaced `src/web/app/globals.css`.
- **Components:** `SiteHeader.tsx` and `SiteFooter.tsx` copied to `src/components/layout/`.
- **Configuration:** `next.config.ts` updated to allow images from `inirestaurant.s3.amazonaws.com`.

### 3. Admin Portal (`src/admin`)
- **Assets:** Logo PNGs copied to `public/images/`.
- **Global CSS:** `globals.css` (branding version) copied to `src/styles/globals.css`.
- **Components:** `AdminSidebar.tsx` and `AdminLayout.tsx` copied to `src/components/layout/`.

### 4. Backend .NET Service (`src/backend/IntegrationService.API`)
- **Constants:** `ImidusConstants.cs` created in `Constants/` folder with brand colors and S3 paths.
- **Email Template:** `BaseEmailTemplate.html` created in `Templates/Email/`.
- **Email Service:** `EmailService.cs` implemented in `Services/` folder (manual creation as not in branding package).
- **Dependency Injection:** `Program.cs` updated to register `SendGrid` and `EmailService`.
- **Configuration:** `appsettings.json` updated with `SendGrid` section (API key placeholder).
- **NuGet Packages:** Added `SendGrid` and `SendGrid.Extensions.DependencyInjection` to `IntegrationService.API.csproj`.
- **Constants Update:** Added `EmailFromName` and `EmailFromAddress` to `ImidusConstants.cs`.

## Notes & Deviations
- **Path Corrections:** Corrected paths from guide (`POSMobile`, `web-ordering`, `admin-portal`) to actual project structure (`src/mobile/ImidusCustomerApp`, `src/web`, `src/admin`).
- **Gold Logo:** `logo-imidus-gold.png` was not present in branding package; used `imidus_logo_blue_gradient.png` as a placeholder.
- **EmailService.cs:** Manually created as it was not included in the branding package file list.
- **File Permissions:** Used `cp -f` to force overwrite read-only files in `src/web/public/images/`.
- **Backend Dependencies:** Added `SendGrid` and `SendGrid.Extensions.DependencyInjection` packages to `IntegrationService.API.csproj`.
- **Backend Constants:** Added `EmailFromName` and `EmailFromAddress` to `ImidusConstants.cs`.

## Next Steps
1.  Verify the SendGrid API key in `appsettings.json` is correct for production.
2.  Upload logo PNGs to S3 bucket `inirestaurant` under `/branding/` (or update `ImidusConstants.cs` if using `/novatech/assets/`).
3.  Run mobile build to verify splash screen and theme.
4.  Run web and admin builds to verify styling.
5.  Test email functionality (requires valid SendGrid API key).
